name: MacTutor
biography:
Eug횄짢ne Rouch횄짤 was born in Sommi횄짢res in southern France. This town, about 30 km from the south coast, is situated between Nimes and Montpellier and now contains a street named after the famous mathematician. His parents were Jean Jacques Rouch횄짤 (1818 - 1861) and Marie Nicot (1807 - 1861) . Jean Jacques Rouch횄짤 was a tax collector and the family moved to Aniane in the H횄짤rault district where Jean Jacques was employed.
Rouch횄짤 completed his schooling at the Sainte-Barbe College in Rue Valette in Paris. This school, founded in 1460 , was designed to prepare students for sitting the entrance examinations for the 횄혟cole Polytechnique and the 횄혟cole Normale Sup횄짤rieure. He attended the school at the same time as Gustave Eiffel (1832 - 1923) , later famed as an engineer. In 1852 , after success in the entrance examinations to the Grandes 횄혟coles, Rouch횄짤 entered the 횄혟cole Polytechnique. When he entered this university, his home address was in Aniane and his physical description is recorded as: light brown hair; low forehead; medium nose; red eyes; medium mouth; round chin; and rounded face. He was in the same class as Alfred de Billy (1832 - 1906) who became an Inspector of Finances, Fernand Raoul-Duval (1833 - 1892) who became a leading engineer, and Fran횄짠ois Chr횄짤tien L횄짤on Hartung (1832 - 1908) who, after a military career, became governor of Verdun. Rouch횄짤 graduated from the 횄혟cole Polytechnique in 1854 after two years of study.
After training as a mathematician, Rouch횄짤 taught for one year at Nantes where he was appointed professor of physics at the Lyc횄짤e de Nantes. Then, in 1855 , he was appointed as professor of mathematics at the Lyc횄짤e Charlemagne in Paris. At this stage Rouch횄짤 had spent only two undergraduate years for his university of mathematics and physics and he had no research degree. He now undertook research and, on 17 July 1858 , he presented two theses to the Faculty of Science in Paris for the degree of Doctor of Mathematical Sciences. The first of these theses was on analysis and was entitled Sur le d횄짤veloppement des fonctions en s횄짤ries ordonn횄짤es suivant les d횄짤nominateurs des r횄짤duites d'une fraction continue 창혫혟 ( On the development of the functions in series ordered according to the denominators of the reductions of a continuous fraction ) while the second was on mechanics and was entitled Sur les int횄짤grales communes 횄혻 plusieurs probl횄짢mes de m횄짤canique relatifs au mouvement d'un point sur une surface 창혫혟 ( On the integrals common to several problems of mechanics relating to the movement of a point on a surface. ) . This second thesis was on a topic which had been studied by Joseph Bertrand . Rouch횄짤 dedicated his two doctoral theses to Auguste Nouseilles (1798 - 1881) , Principal of the Lyc횄짤e Charlemagne. An oral examination was held on 8 November 1858 with Louis Lef횄짤bure de Fourcy as President, and Gabriel Lam횄짤 and Joseph Liouville as examiners. Rouch횄짤 was awarded his doctorate but we have to feel a little sorry for him since Lef횄짤bure de Fourcy had a reputation as a difficult examiner. An inspector who sat in on several of the examinations conducted by Lef횄짤bure de Fourcy wrote:- Several times I attended oral examinations by Mr Lef횄짤bure de Fourcy and this is my impression. The professor cannot be considered a benevolent reviewer, his voice is harsh, his tone is dismissive, also he questioned in a very firm stiff manner, and showed no mercy for any errors of logic. In 1858 Rouch횄짤 was appointed as admissions examiner for the 횄혟cole Centrale in Paris, a position he held until 1877 . He was appointed as a tutor for geometry and stereotomy for the 횄혟cole Polytechnique in 1861 , continuing to hold this position for the next 22 years. Rouch횄짤 married Suzanne H횄짤l횄짢ne Marie Bouzanquet on 11 December 1861 in Lunel, H횄짤rault. H횄짤l횄짢ne Marie Bouzanquet had been born 26 September 1838 in Lunel. They had a son Louis Eug횄짢ne Rouch횄짤 (1862 - 1957) who was known as "Jacques". He became an Inspector of Finance, and Director of the National Academy of Music and Dance from 1914 to 1944 .
In 1867 Rouch횄짤 was nominated professor of descriptive geometry and stereotomy at the 횄혟cole Centrale in Paris. He also acted as an examiner at the 횄혟cole Polytechnique from 1877 to 1883 . In 1884 he was named professor holding the chair of descriptive geometry at the Conservatoire des Arts et M횄짤tiers in Paris. He continued to hold this position until he retired in 1905 .
Rouch횄짤 published many mathematics articles, among them were some which appeared in Comptes Rendus and some in the Journal of the 횄혟cole Polytechnique . For example he published M횄짤moire sur la serie de Lagrange 창혫혟 ( Memoir on the series of Lagrange ) in the second of these two journals in 1862 . In Comptes Rendus he published papers such as Sur la discussion des 횄짤quations du premier degr횄짤 창혫혟 ( On the discussion of first degree equations ) (1875) , Sur les lignes asymptotiques d'une surface du quatri횄짢me degr횄짤 창혫혟 ( On the asymptotic lines of a surface of the fourth degree ) (1877) , Sur un probl횄짢me relatif 횄혻 la dur횄짤e du jeu 창혫혟 ( On a problem with the duration of the game ) (1888) , and Sur la formule de Stirling 창혫혟 ( On the Stirling formula ) (1890) . The two-page paper Sur la discussion des equations du premier degr횄짤 창혫혟 ( On the discussion of equations of the first degree ) in volume 81 of Comptes Rendus of the Acad횄짤mie des Sciences contains his result on solving systems of linear equations. This is the well-known criterion which says that a system of linear equations has a solution if and only if the rank of the matrix of the associated homogeneous system is equal to the rank of the augumented matrix of the system. Rouch횄짤 later published a fuller version of this theorem in 1880 in the Journal de l'횄혟cole Polytechnique . In fact he was not the first to prove such a result and, after Rouch횄짤's paper appeared, Georges Fonten횄짤 published a note in the Nouvelles Annales de Math횄짤matiques claiming priority. When Georg Frobenius discussed this result in his papers, for example in Zur Theorie der linearen Gleichungen 창혫혟 ( On the theory of linear equations ) published in Crelle's Journal in 1905 , he gave credit for proving the theorem to both Rouch횄짤 and Fonten횄짤. However it is now often called the Rouch횄짤- Frobenius theorem, especially in the Spanish speaking world. This is almost certainly because the Spanish/Argentinian mathematician Julio Rey Pastor referred to the theorem by this name.
Although few today know who Rouch횄짤 was, his name is very well known through 'Rouch횄짤's theorem' which he published in volume 39 of the Journal of the 횄혟cole Polytechnique in 1862 :- If f ( z ) and g ( z ) are two complex functions which are regular within and on a closed contour C, on which f ( z ) does not vanish and also 창혞짙 g ( z ) 창혞짙 < 창혞짙 f ( z ) 창혞짙 |g(z)| < |f (z)| 창혞짙 g ( z ) 창혞짙 < 창혞짙 f ( z ) 창혞짙 then f ( z ) f (z) f ( z ) and f ( z ) + g ( z ) f (z) + g(z) f ( z ) + g ( z ) have the same number of zeros within C. Rouch횄짤 also wrote several textbooks including El횄짤ments d'alg횄짢bre 횄혻 l'usage des candidats au baccalaur횄짤at 횄짤s sciences et aux 횄짤coles sp횄짤ciales 창혫혟 ( Elements of algebra for candidates for the baccalaureate of science and special schools ) (1857) , Le횄짠ons nouvelles de trigonom횄짤trie rectiligne et sph횄짤rique 창혫혟 ( New lessons in rectilinear and spherical trigonometry ) ( written jointly with L Lacour ) (1857) , Trait횄짤 de g횄짤om횄짤trie 횄짤l횄짤mentaire 창혫혟 ( Treatise on elementary geometry ) ( written jointly with Charles de Comberousse ) (1864 - 1866) , 횄혟l횄짤ments de Statique Graphique 창혫혟 ( Elements of graphic statics ) (1889) , Coupe des pierres: pr횄짤c횄짤d횄짤e des principes du trait de st횄짤r횄짤otomie 창혫혟 ( Cutting stones: preceded by the principles of the theory of stereotomy ) ( written jointly with Charles Brisse ) (1893) , and Analyse infinit횄짤simale 횄혻 l'usage des ing횄짤nieurs 창혫혟 ( Infinitesimal analysis for engineers ) (2 volumes ) (1900 - 1902) . This two volume work was the result of a collaboration with Lucien L횄짤vy (1853 - 1912) who was the father of the well known mathematician Paul L횄짤vy . Lucien L횄짤vy taught mathematics at the Lyc횄짤e Louis-le-Grand in Paris and, for 23 years, was an examiner at the 횄혟cole Polytechnique.
His most famous textbook was the geometry book written jointly with Charles Jules F횄짤lix de Comberousse (1826 - 1897) who taught mathematics at the Coll횄짢ge Chaptal in Paris. This book appeared in two volumes, the first being on G횄짤om횄짤trie plane 창혫혟 ( Plane geometry ) and the second on G횄짤om횄짤trie dans l'espace 창혫혟 ( Geometry in space ) . The book went through many editions, for example the seventh edition of this work was published in Paris by Gauthier-Villars in 1900 . It was a major treatise, the volumes having 548 and 664 pages respectively. Editions continued to be published after Rouch횄짤's death, with a new edition published in Paris by Gauthier-Villars in 1922 and another in 1954 . Indeed it is still in print now with a reproduction produced in 2010 . The authors describe their approach to writing this textbook in the following way:- There are two ways of writing a book intended for study: one can restrict oneself to the official syllabus and do not go outside the framework; or perhaps one can also, by strictly following these syllabuses in what they require to be compulsory, yet go beyond and try to complete them. In order to apply a science, it is not sufficient to know a few parts of it, rather it is necessary to be familiar with all its methods, to grasp the whole. The magnificent discoveries of modern geometry have not penetrated into teaching; abandoned by the official syllabuses, they do not occupy the place in the series of mathematical studies due to them; we speak of it only a little and incidentally in Analytical Geometry, where it seems to be wrong to replace the admirable ideas created by Descartes by new ideas. We are far from reproaching the syllabuses for their silence in this respect; they are so full that it would be unworthy to demand an addition. But can one not learn the syllabus required for an examination and try at the same time to understand the scope of the science that is being studied, by making with a quick study, an overview of its main methods? Such is the thought which guided us in the composition of this work. For a list of the contents of the 1900 edition of this work, which clearly exhibits the philosophy described by its authors in the above quote, see THIS LINK . Edmond Laguerre died in 1886 and Rouch횄짤 became one of the editors to work on the production of his Collected Works . There were three editors for this work, the other two being Charles Hermite and Henri Poincar횄짤 . Oeuvres de Laguerre Tome I, Alg횄짢bre, calcul integral 창혫혟 ( Works of Laguerre Vol I, Algebra, calculus integral ) appeared in 1898 and Oeuvres de Laguerre Tome II, G횄짤om횄짤trie 창혫혟 ( Works of Laguerre Vol II, Geometry ) was published in 1905 .
Rouch횄짤 was a member of the Soci횄짤t횄짤 Math횄짤matique de France , serving as its president in 1883 - 84 . He was also a member of the Soci횄짤t횄짤 philomathique in Paris and a member of the Conseil sup횄짤rieur de l'enseignement technique . He was elected to the Academy of Sciences in 1896 and to the Acad횄짤mie des Sciences et Lettres de Montpellier.
The town of Lunel where Rouch횄짤 died is close to his birthplace of Sommi횄짢res. In fact it is situated about halfway between Sommi횄짢res and the southern coast of France.
